articles.title: Historic Vance-Ghalibaf talks must bridge deep distrust
articles.brief: Iranian President Ebrahim Raisi's chief of staff, Mohammad Jamshidi, has been in talks with Iranian parliament speaker Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf, who is also in contact with US officials, including US Speaker of the House Kevin McCarthy's chief of staff, Andy Biggs. The talks aim to facilitate a potential meeting between Ghalibaf and US Speaker of the House Kevin McCarthy, marking the highest-level engagement between Iran and the US since the 1979 Islamic Revolution.

articles.title: Final push for votes as challenger to Hungary's Orbán scents victory
articles.brief: Hungary's opposition movement, led by Péter Márki-Zay, is currently leading in the polls ahead of the country's election. Tens of thousands of anti-Orbán supporters gathered in Budapest's Heroes' Square to show support for the opposition movement.

articles.title: Lebanon and Israel officials to meet in US on Tuesday
articles.brief: Lebanon and Israel officials are scheduled to meet in the US on Tuesday for talks. The meeting is set to take place despite a previous condition from Lebanon that a ceasefire must be in place for them to participate.

articles.title: Palestinian shot dead during Israeli settler attack on occupied West Bank village
articles.brief: A Palestinian man was shot dead during an attack by Israeli settlers on a village in the occupied West Bank. The incident occurred amid warnings from former security chiefs that "government-sponsored Jewish terrorism" is escalating in the region.

articles.title: Man accused of coercing wife into sex with 120 men goes on trial in Sweden
articles.brief: A 61-year-old man in Sweden has gone on trial, accused of coercing his wife into having sex with 120 men. The man denies allegations that he used threats of physical violence and drugs to force his wife into these encounters.

articles.title: Dancer with MND performs on stage again through digital avatar
articles.brief: Breanna Olson, a dancer with motor neuron disease (MND), used brainwave technology to perform on stage through a digital avatar. The technology allowed her to re-establish the expression and connection she felt had been lost due to her condition.

articles.title: Meta pulls Facebook ads recruiting for social media addiction lawsuits
articles.brief: Meta has removed Facebook ads promoting lawsuits against the company for social media addiction, following a recent court ruling in California. The ads were likely targeted at individuals who may have developed addiction to Facebook or other Meta platforms.

articles.title: White House staff told not to place bets on prediction markets
articles.brief: The White House has instructed its staff to refrain from participating in prediction markets, citing concerns over potential conflicts of interest. The directive aims to prevent staff from making bets on events that may be influenced by their official duties or access to sensitive information.

articles.title: Mayor warns of London 'disinformation blizzard'
articles.brief: London Mayor Sir Sadiq Khan has warned of a "disinformation blizzard" targeting the city, claiming it is being portrayed as being in decline. The disinformation is allegedly being spread to mislead the public about the city's actual state.

articles.title: Amazon to end support for older Kindles, prompting user outcry
articles.brief: Amazon will discontinue support for older Kindles released before 2013, affecting users' ability to download new e-books. This change means owners of pre-2013 Kindles will no longer be able to access new e-book content from Amazon.

articles.title: Artemis II splashdown: Astronauts return to Earth after lunar mission
articles.brief: The Artemis II crew successfully splashed down in the Pacific Ocean after completing a lunar mission, marking a key test of the spacecraft's heat shield during re-entry. The astronauts landed safely under parachutes, bringing an end to the mission that tested the capabilities of the spacecraft.

articles.title: Iran war live: US negotiators due to arrive in Pakistan for ceasefire talks
articles.brief: US negotiators are scheduled to arrive in Pakistan for ceasefire talks. The Lebanese Health Ministry reports 1,953 people have been killed and 6,303 wounded in Israeli attacks since March 2.
